revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:幡日佐神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Hatahisa Shrine (幡日佐神社) |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = File:Hatahisa-jinja keidai.JPG |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|Location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|qid=Q170421|lt=Nantan City|1=Nantan, Kyoto}} Yagi-cho, Himoro Nakamotoyama 11-73 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|41.95|N|135|33|12.94|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Hatahisa Shrine (幡日佐神社)}} |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|Deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
Himuro no Mikoto |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|Type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small)
Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Founding = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate name = Hatahisa Himuro Dual Shrine |Ema, etc. = |Religious affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|Festival = {{ill|October 21|en|October 21|qid=Q2960|lt=October 21|1=October 21}} |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Nantan|qid=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.094986|N|135.553594|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} {{Coordinates list}} [[File:Hatahisa-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Hatahisa Shrine''' (幡日佐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|qid=Q170421|lt=Nantan City|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Former Shrine Rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Because the Himuro Shrine that once existed in Kamiyoshi was merged into this establishment, it is also referred to as “'''Hatahisa Himuro Dual Shrine'''”The name “Hatahisa Himuro Dual Shrine” appears, for example, on the horizontal plaque of the torii. In this article, the registered name by the Association of Shinto Shrines, “Hatahisa Shrine”, is followed.. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * '''Hondawake no Mikoto''' (品陀別命) *: Same as {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. He has been the enshrined deity since the founding of the shrine. * '''Himuro no Mikoto''' (氷室命) *: The deity originally venerated at the Himuro Shrine in Kamiyoshi, merged during the Edo period. == History ==
Chronology of Hatahisa Shrine
{{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|02|03| 01='''［Kamiyoshi Shimura］'''
(Yagi, Kamiyoshi, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|qid=Q170421|lt=Nantan City|1=Nantan, Kyoto}})|02='''［Himoro Village (Former Shrine)］'''
(Himoro, Yagi, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|qid=Q170421|lt=Nantan City|1=Nantan, Kyoto}})|03='''［Himoro Village (Current Location)］'''
(Himoro, Yagi, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|qid=Q170421|lt=Nantan City|1=Nantan, Kyoto}})}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01=Himuro Daimyōjin
(Himuro Shrine)}} {{familytree|border=0||||||)|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0||||||!|01| |01=Himoro Daimyōjin}} {{familytree|border=0||||||!||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||!|02| 01=(traditionally) during the {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|qid=Q1373026|lt=Wado|1=Wadō (era)}} period (708–715)|02=Hataku Daimyōjin}} {{familytree|border=0||||||!||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||!|02| 01={{ill|Enchō|ja|延長 (日本)|lt=Enchō|lt_ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延長 (元号)|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1327274}} 5th year (927)
‘{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja|神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}’|02=Hatahisa Shrine}} {{familytree|border=0||||||!||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02||!| 01=(traditionally) {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}|02=(burned down)}} {{familytree|border=0||||||:||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||:||`|-|02| 01=(traditionally) {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 (1591)|02=(relocated)}} {{familytree|border=0||||||:||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||L|02|[| 01={{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}|02=(merged)}} {{familytree|border=0||||||||||01| 01=Himuro Hataku Ryōsha Daimyōjin}} {{familytree|border=0||||||||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|||||||02| 01={{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1876)|02='''Hatahisa Shrine'''
(Hatahisa Himuro Dual Shrine)}} {{familytree|border=0||||||!||!}} {{familytree/end}}
=== Founding === Once, beyond the mountain pass behind the shrine, in Kamiyoshi Shimura (present-day {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|qid=Q170421|lt=Nantan City|1=Nantan, Kyoto}} Yagi, Kamiyoshi), it is said that the Imperial Court ({{ill|Imperial Court|ja|朝廷 (日本)|lt=Imperial Court|lt_ja|朝廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Imperial Court of Japan|qid=Q30253177}}) established a {{ill|Ice house (building)|en|Ice house (building)|qid=Q253893|lt=Himuro|1=Ice house (building)}} – a facility for storing ice to be offered in summer. According to shrine tradition, the deity “Himuro Daimyōjin” was enshrined there. Later, at an uncertain time, a separate shrine was erected at Himoro, marking the founding of this establishment. Initially, it was built on the site of the former shrine (old shrine) located in the southern part of the current precincts and was known as “Himoro Daimyōjin.” === Overview === In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the 'Engishiki' ({{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|lt=Jinmyōchō|lt_ja|神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}) records the Hatahisa Shrine in {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}}’s Funai District ({{ill|Funai District, Kyoto|en|Funai District, Kyoto|qid=Q1078679|lt=Funai District|1=Funai District, Kyoto}}), listing it as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. Similarly, the 'Engishiki' lists ten Himuro shrines, among which the Himuro at “Ikebe” in {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}}’s Kuwata District ({{ill|Kuwata District|ja|桑田郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwata District|1=Kuwada district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11537600}}) is identified with the local Himuro.[{{NDLDC|991103/539}} ‘Kokushi Taikei Vol. 13’] (Keizai Zasshisha, 1897–1901, National Diet Library Digital Collection) Plate 539.‘Kokushi Daijiten’ (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) – Entry on Himuro. Furthermore, this Himuro also served as the setting for the Noh play {{ill|Yōkyoku|en|Yōkyoku|qid=Q8062662|lt=Noh play|1=Yōkyoku}} “Himuro.” In the 19th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1591), the shrine buildings were constructed after relocating from the site of the former shrine. As the Himuro Daimyōjin of Kamiyoshi Shimura had been burned down amid the conflicts of the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}, in the 9th year of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} (1669) the same deity was enshrined in unison, and thereafter it was known as “Himuro Hataku Ryōsha Daimyōjin.” In the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1873), under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Former Shrine Rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. In the 9th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1876), based on its status as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the shrine was renamed “Hatahisa Shrine”. The shrine building burned down in the 10th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} (1921) and was rebuilt in the 13th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} (1924). == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 13 (1924) in the {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style, featuring a central gable. * Worship Hall * Chōzuya – Utilizing spring water from the rear mountain. == Auxiliary Shrines == * Wakamiya Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. * Awashima Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}}. * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama|1=Ukanomitama}}. * Suwa Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|lt=Suwa Daimyōjin|1=Takeminakata}}. == Related Sites == [[File:Hatahisa-jinja furumiya.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Ruins of the Former Shrine]] * Ruins of the Former Shrine (Himoro, Yagi, {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|qid=Q170421|lt=Nantan City|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, {{ウィキ座標|35|5|20.50|N|135|33|18.50|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|位置|name=Ruins of the Former Shrine}}) – A stone monument inscribed “Ruins of the Former Shrine” stands there. == Festivals == * Ininen Festival ({{ill|February 11|en|February 11|qid=Q2335|lt=February 11|1=February 11}}) * Awashima Festival ({{ill|April 3|en|April 3|qid=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}}, {{ill|September 3|en|September 3|qid=Q2860|lt=September 3|1=September 3}}) * Himuro Festival ({{ill|July 1|en|July 1|qid=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}}) * Annual Festival ({{ill|October 21|en|October 21|qid=Q2960|lt=October 21|1=October 21}}) – A marital ritual is held with Ōsō Shrine in the neighboring Hioki district. * Niiname-sai ({{ill|November 23|en|November 23|qid=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}}) == Local Information == '''Location''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|qid=Q170421|lt=Nantan City|1=Nantan, Kyoto}} Yagi-cho, Himoro Nakamotoyama 11-73 '''Transportation Access''' * From {{ill|Yagi Station|en|Yagi Station|qid=Q1043724|lt=Yagi Station|1=Yagi Station}}, {{ill|Yoshitomi Station (Kyoto)|en|Yoshitomi Station (Kyoto)|qid=Q1044085|lt=Yoshitomi Station|1=Yoshitomi Station (Kyoto)}}, and {{ill|Sonobe Station|en|Sonobe Station|qid=Q1044120|lt=Sonobe Station|1=Sonobe Station}} (all on {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}’s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|Sagano Line|qid=Q1073011|lt=Sagano Line|1=Sagano Line}}), take the {{ill|Keihan Kyoto Transportation|ja|京阪京都交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Kyoto Transportation|1=Keihan Kyoto Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11375832}} bus to the “Nishisho” bus stop (approximately a 10‐minute walk). '''Surroundings''' * Ōsō Shrine – Known for the {{ill|Ōsō Shrine Tug-of-War|ja|大送神社の綱引き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsō Shrine Tug-of-War|1=Ōsō Shrine Tug-of-War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11439317}}. * Himuro no Sato (Nantan City Yagi Agricultural Environment Park) == References == {{Citation Help}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ‘Japan Historical Place Names Encyclopedia: Kyoto Prefecture Place Names’ ({{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}) – Hatahisa Shrine entry == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Hatahisa-jinja}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing06.html#hatahisahimurojinja Hatahisa Himuro Dual Shrine] (Nantan Life – Nantan City Comprehensive Guide) {{Shinto Navbox}} {{translated page|ja|幡日佐神社|version=97912278|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:幡日佐神社]] {{Commons category|Hatahisa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11481867}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Himuro Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nantan, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
